pages.full_text:
chain-smoked throughout. Naomi worked out she must spend £4,000 a year on ciggies.

   In fact it was always difficult to find a space on deck where there wasn't cigarette smoke. And the people were fat fat FAT. Even the cabaret singer was FAT. We had just seen Wall-e so we ought to know.

   The next day we took it in turns to look after Austin, Simon, Charles & I, so that Thomas & Naomi could go to a movie. We saw dolphins, but poor Naomi didn't see them, or a whale.

   __Tuesday__
   At a truly horrendous early hour (6) we had breakfast & left the ferry at 7. We meandered along looking at the plains at Amurria we stopped & walked around. We shopped at a supermarket & had lunch in Burgos in the country side in shade outside someone's deserted house.

   On to a campsite across the river from Burgos. Simon sent a text to Thomas to see
pages.notes: Page continues from a previous spread about a ferry crossing (likely Santander/Bilbao route to Spain). Wall-E was released in 2008, strongly suggesting this is a 2008 trip. 'Amurria' may be Amurrio (town in Basque Country, Spain) — written as 'Amurria' in the diary. The handwritten page number is not visible at the top-left of this image; the text appears to begin mid-narrative continued from the previous page.

pages.full_text:
how they were - they had got no further than a garage near the ferry because they had a leak! Fortunately, so far, the garage has welded a pipe with a hole in it near the radiator, & they may be on the road again when it cools.

   We've been swimming.

   I forgot to mention that after leaving Venetia Thomas put a lead-free petrol nozzle in his tank & was only reminded at the last minute by Naomi that he ought to be using diesel. Happy Holidays!

   __Wednesday July 30th__
   We drove off to Burgos & parked near the river. There was [illegible sketch/drawing] a beautiful metal sculpture.

   In a big square we had lunch - pizza for €6, chicken & chips & salad €6.50 & veal & chips. While I was watching 2 small children ran about on a metal stage, watched by their
pages.notes: Page number 77 visible at top-right. 'Venetia' is likely a place name (possibly a campsite or town visited before the ferry). Wednesday 30 July fell in 2008, consistent with the Wall-E reference on the left page. A small hand-drawn sketch (lines suggesting a sculpture or monument) appears inline in the text — not a route map. The text is cut off at the bottom continuing to the next spread.
